{"problem_standardized_math":"Let \(J(x)=U\Sigma V^T\) be the singular-value decomposition of \(J(x)\). For the solution \(p\) of\n\[\n(J^T J+\lambda I)\,p=-J^T r,\n\tag{10.33}\n\]\nexpress \(p\) in terms of the singular values and singular vectors of \(J(x)\) and the scalar \(\lambda\). Also express its squared norm \(\|p\|^2\) in these terms, and show that\n\[\n\lim_{\lambda\to 0} p=\sum_{\sigma_i\ne 0}\frac{u_i^T r}{\sigma_i}\,v_i.\n\]"}
